definition: JPY carry unwind is a validated mechanism with speculative exposure of ~$300-500B (not the $4-6T headline which includes banks, corporates, and naturally hedged positions). August 2024 validated the mechanism (USD/JPY 162→142, Nikkei -12%, VIX >65). CFTC positioning has rebuilt to ~120K contracts (~60-70% of pre-August peaks) and carry cushion compressed from ~5.5% to ~3.5-4.0%.
evidence: challenger_01 decomposition of $4-6T into speculative ($300-500B) vs. hedged survived debate
CFTC positioning rebuilt to ~60-70% of pre-August peaks
BoJ capitulated after August 2024 stress — circuit-breaker function, not resilience
pair_3 refuted resilience framing: BoJ intervention made the test pass
Six agents engaged this topic — most broadly analyzed claim
open_questions: Is the BoJ circuit-breaker (willingness to pause normalization) durable?
What credit cycle conditions would produce systemic rather than contained unwind?
Has market structure changed enough since August 2024 to alter unwind dynamics?
